channel.title: Design for Manufacture
channel.description: Essays on engineering, technology, and communist politics

item.title: The Immortal Science: Preface to the French Translation of “Forest and Factory”
Interview with a Medical Device Engineer
item.description: Pleased to share the transcription for an interview I did for Vital Signs magazine, a publication by and for hospital workers in Bristol, UK. We touched on numerous topics, including global supply chains, the abstraction of hospital labor into monetary concerns, engineer and scientists subjectivities, worker organizing, medical device design paradigms, speculation about communism, and more.

item.title: Engineers, Materialism, and the Communist Method
item.description: My new essay Engineers, Materialism, and the Communist Method can now be read at Notes From Below.

item.title: A Tale of Two Engineer-Artisans
item.description: I recently met two interesting individuals. The context in which I met each of them were remarkably similar: each was running a small business selling a specialized boutique commodity. The first was a man operating a small vineyard and winery on the property of his exurban home. The second was a man running a coffee roasting operation out of a small warehouse in an industrial park. Both were produ

item.title: Review: “Everything for Everyone: An Oral History of the New York Commune 2052-2072” by M.E. O’Brien and Eman Abdelhadi
item.description: Speculative fiction is a diverse genre capable of telling stories that may tickle the imagination and spark hope for a better tomorrow, or simply reify the most insipid aspects of modern society and push reactionary stupidity. Everything for Everyone: An Oral History of the New York Commune, 2052-2072 by M.E. O’Brien and Eman Abdelhadi is certainly the first kind. The book is structured as a serie

item.title: Productive Distribution: Speculation on Communist Manufacturing by Volume
item.description: Elsewhere I have written on the distinction between low mix high volume manufacturing and high mix low volume manufacturing and how this dichotomy might play into a communist reconfiguration of capitalist industry, but in that essay I did not do much to discuss how this factors into communist society in the long term.
